### Handover Run

Operational handover activities required to transition a Teams Phone solution from delivery to RUN.

#### Purpose

Ensure that RUN support can understand, monitor, change and restore the delivered Teams Phone scope after the build team disengages.

#### Handover artefacts

- architecture or service diagrams
- call-flow documentation
- number inventory and routing overview
- Auto Attendant and Call Queue inventory
- Resource Account mapping
- user and service-number scope
- evidence pack
- operational contacts and escalation paths
- known limitations and accepted risks
- routine change guidance

#### Minimum support knowledge

Support should know:

- what was delivered
- which numbers and services are in scope
- which Teams objects are involved
- which operator / SBC boundary applies
- how to triage common symptoms
- where evidence and configuration summaries are stored
- which changes are standard and which require design review

#### Handover sequence

1. Review delivered scope.
2. Review call flows and routing dependencies.
3. Review evidence and validation results.
4. Review known limitations and accepted risks.
5. Confirm routine operational changes.
6. Confirm incident escalation boundaries.
7. Confirm ownership after handover.

#### Evidence expectations

- handover checklist
- validation results
- evidence pack reference
- known issue register
- support owner mapping
- escalation boundary summary
- sign-off or acknowledgement record

#### Acceptance criteria

- support can identify the delivered objects
- support can triage common incidents
- standard changes are understood
- escalation paths are known
- the service can leave project mode and enter RUN mode
